_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication of procedure and planned line of management
Barium X-ray / Upper GI Endoscopy / USG/CECT/MRI Abdomen / Meckel scan (99mTcpertechnetate Scintigraphy – gastrointestinal bleeding) / Double balloon Enteroscopy

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned lined of treatment, indication for procedure?
Did clinical presentation and imaging confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes with photographs available?
Was the imaging indicative of surgery?
Was the histopathological examination report available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical presentation or imaging not indicative of surgery.
Missing or incomplete clinical notes at pre-authorization.
Missing or incomplete imaging reports (Barium X-ray/Upper GI Endoscopy/USG/CECT/MRI Abdomen) at pre-authorization.
Surgery performed for asymptomatic duodenal diverticulum without other indications (e.g., bleeding).
Missing detailed procedure / operative notes at claim submission.
Missing histopathological examination report at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Duodenal diverticula are the most common type of small bowel diverticula. It is a pouch attached to the duodenum. There are 2 types of duodenal diverticulum – Intramural and Extramural.

Extramural diverticulum is the more common type that sticks out from duodenum and varies in size. They are acquired as this is secondary to herniation near the entrance of a large vessel. They usually are located in the area around the Papilla of Vater.
Intramural diverticulum (windsock diverticula) is of rare type that protrudes into the duodenal lumen. This is congenital, secondary to incomplete canalization.
contraindications: Asymptomatic duodenal diverticulum incidentally detected during imaging does not require any surgical intervention.
